# Nuthatch

> A self-hosted-first, AI-native blockchain indexer in one Rust binary. From a contract
> address to a live indexed API, with no mandatory third-party data service and nothing
> phoning home.

Nuthatch indexes EVM chains on your own hardware. Embedded mode runs as a single static
binary with no external services: no Postgres, no Docker, no IPFS. It is free and licensed
AGPL-3.0. Status: pre-release. This file separates what the binary does today from the design
targets, so a model does not present unbuilt features as present-tense fact.

Tagline: "Be your own indexer."

## What it does today

- `nuthatch init 0xAddr --chain mainnet` resolves the ABI (Sourcify, then an Etherscan-class
  API) and scaffolds a project: `nuthatch.toml`, `abi.json`, an `llms.txt`, and a Claude Code
  skill. It does not yet generate schemas, views, handlers, or tests.
- `nuthatch dev` polls logs over RPC (round-robin failover), decodes ERC-20 `Transfer` events,
  stores them in an embedded hot store (redb), seals finalized ranges to content-addressed
  Parquet, and serves a local HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:8288: `/entities`, `/entity/{id}`, `/sql`
  (read-only DuckDB over sealed data), `/balances`, `/balance/{addr}`.
- Balances are an incrementally-maintained view (DBSP); a reorg is a retraction.
- `nuthatch transform <component.wasm>` runs a pure, capability-isolated WASM transform over a
  batch (Arrow). `nuthatch mcp` serves the Model Context Protocol over stdio (tools: status,
  schema, sql, entity, balance, top_balances), bridging offline to a running instance.

## Design targets (not built yet)

- Under 2 minutes from `init` to first query; at most 2 GB RAM for a single chain (a CI budget
  enforces a 256 MB ceiling in tests today).
- reth ExEx tip-ingestion against your own node (designed and stubbed; RPC is the working path).
- Multi-contract "nests", user-authored SQL views, generated schemas + tests from an ABI, a
  GraphQL layer, streaming subscribe, scaled mode (Postgres + DataFusion), and local AI models
  (Ollama / bring-your-own-key).

## How it works

- Ingestion (RPC extraction today; reth ExEx when colocated, designed) -> deterministic decode
  -> a hot store (redb) for point-reads at the tip -> content-addressed Parquet sealed past
  finality -> embedded DuckDB attached read-only for analytical SQL. Balances are declarative
  incremental views (IVM).
- Reorgs touch only the mutable hot store and become retractions in the incremental view. The
  columnar layer is append-only by construction.
- The escape hatch is capability-isolated WASM components via WIT. A component granted zero
  capabilities is a pure function and can be re-executed and verified; its purity is checkable
  from its imports alone.

## Principles

- Single static binary; zero external services in embedded mode.
- No phone-home: no telemetry, no mandatory API tokens, no gated data service, ever.
- Deterministic core: decode, reorg handling, and entity derivation are reproducible. LLMs
  generate code and tests; LLM output never sits in the runtime data path.
- AI-native today: a built-in MCP server, a `schema` tool, and a shipped `llms.txt` and Claude
  Code skill, all offline against your local instance. Local models (Ollama / BYO-key) are planned.

## Install (pre-release)

- No published binaries yet; the shell installer is a placeholder that prints a notice.
- Build from source: `cargo install nuthatch` (the crate name is reserved; publish is imminent).
- Homebrew tap and the Docker (scaled-mode) stack are planned.

## Worked example (design preview)

`/example` is a design preview: it describes how a real community Graph Protocol subgraph
(PaulieB14/horizon-indexer-subgraph, credited to Paul Barba) would be rebuilt as a Nuthatch
"nest" — Graph Horizon staking on Arbitrum One across three contracts, one `init` over several
addresses, raw event tables queryable during backfill, AssemblyScript handlers rebuilt as
declarative incremental views (reorgs retract; no rollback code), generated tests, and serving
over SQL, GraphQL, SSE, and MCP. This is the target design, not the current binary, which indexes
a single contract's `Transfer` events with an incremental balance view.

## Who runs it (design preview)

`/stories` answers "is this for me?" through three illustrative personas (not testimonials),
each mapped to one of the three front doors: an SQL endpoint for application backends,
published nests for analysts who write no indexing code, and the built-in MCP server for
agents. Like `/example`, it is written in the present tense for readability but describes the
product as designed: multi-contract `init` and MCP work today, while user-authored views,
`init --from`, non-`Transfer` decode, and chains other than mainnet are design targets. It
notes that derived views read finalized data while raw event tables are fresh to the tip, and
that a GraphQL layer for subgraph-shaped clients is on the roadmap.

## Roadmap

`/roadmap` is generated from the source repo, not from marketing copy: the README's working-now
table and progress log are the source of truth for what is shipped, and the numbered RFCs under
`docs/rfcs/` for what is planned. Shipped: the embedded pipeline end to end, nests, three chains
(Ethereum mainnet + Arbitrum One + Base), the measured ~20× seal-direct/pipelined backfill
(RFC-0004), the operator surface (`/metrics`, `/sql` guards, graceful shutdown), and the tagged
v0.1.0 release on crates.io with prebuilt binaries. In progress: the Horizon nest parity fixtures
(RFC-0002) and reth ExEx wiring (RFC-0003, needs a synced node). Planned: the remaining v0.1.x
release engineering (RFC-0005), factory/dynamic discovery (RFC-0009), the graph-network nest and
Lodestar migration (RFC-0011), a local admin UI + webhooks (RFC-0010), the compliance pack
(RFC-0008), and deferred items marked "RFC to come". Not planned: a token or decentralized-network
features, telemetry, a Kubernetes-first deployment, and any gateway/auth/metering layer in the
core. It promises order, not dates, and defers to the README progress log when the two disagree.

- Design preview (subgraph to nest): https://www.nuthatch-indexer.com/example

- Roadmap: https://www.nuthatch-indexer.com/roadmap
- Manifesto: https://www.nuthatch-indexer.com/manifesto
- Source and docs: https://github.com/cargopete/nuthatch
